title: Anti-infective and therapeutic electromagnetic emission methods and devices
abstract: A light emitting device for video displays with anti-infective and therapeutic electromagnetic emission methods and devices is disclosed. The light emitting device for video displays with anti-infective and therapeutic electromagnetic emission methods and devices uses non-visible and/or visible electromagnetic radiation on living species, including but not limited to people and animals, to provide treatments against infections and/or provide photobiomodulation treatments to improve health and wellness.
claims: 1. An electronic device for providing photobiomodulation (“PBM”) light therapy towards a face of a person facing the electronic device, the electronic device comprising: a housing; at least one video display device; at least one sensor configured to sense a distance between the electronic device and the face of the person facing the electronic device; a first plurality of light emitters configured to receive first control signals that cause the first plurality of light emitters to emit modulated RGB wavelengths of light for producing video images on the at least one video display device; and a second plurality of light emitters configured to receive second control signals in response to the at least one sensor sensing the distance between the electronic device and the face of the person, wherein the second control signals cause the second plurality of light emitters to emit PBM wavelengths of light at a first output energy level for a duration of time for providing PBM light therapy towards the face of the person facing the electronic device, wherein the PBM wavelengths of light emitted comprise one or more PBM wavelengths of light within a range of 600 nm to 1400 nm known to stimulate mitochondria cells in a person and cause the mitochondria cells to produce additional adenosine triphosphate (“ATP”), wherein the second plurality of light emitters is configured to be controlled to emit the PBM wavelengths of light independent of the first plurality of light emitters being modulated and producing any video images, wherein the second plurality of light emitters is further controlled to adjust the first output energy level or the duration of time the PBM wavelengths of light are emitted in response to changes in the distance sensed between the face of the person and the electronic device by the at least one sensor, and wherein the second plurality of light emitters is configured to be controlled in response to an output from the at least one sensor and emit light at the first output energy level for the duration of time or a second output energy level or a second duration of time before twelve noon according to a geographical location of the electronic device.
